scope_boundary: Bounded deterministic pseudo-random sample for local repository attestation over four selected public-safe result artifacts from a six-artifact candidate pool. The audit compares filesystem bytes, git object bytes, and command replay for each selected artifact, rejects eight mutation controls, and records explicit attestation limits. It is not a deployed attestation service, not open-world receipt faithfulness, not external review, not verifier-correctness evidence, and not an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The randomized attestation audit is useful because it moves the record-reality lane beyond one selected artifact: a reproducible deterministic sample checks four public-safe repository result artifacts through separate filesystem, git-object, and command-replay routes while trap, same-component self-check, unbounded-attestation, and support-promotion controls remain rejected. The evidence remains local and bounded, so it blocks promotion for deployed attestation, open-world receipt faithfulness, verifier correctness, external project truth, deployed audit behavior, or chapter-core support.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable attestation service trace
public-safe produced artifacts with parent jobs, source/context sets, context transactions, semantic certificates, replay declarations, residual declarations, evidence gates, and claim/test links
independent attestation route outside the producing component
trap receipt or challenge suite executed outside the attested component
external review of verifier-quality and attestation limits
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: filesystem digest mismatch in the selected sample is rejected
git object digest mismatch in the selected sample is rejected
command replay failure in the selected sample is rejected
support promotion from randomized attestation shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: single-artifact sample treated as randomized audit
same-component self-check accepted as independent attestation
support-state promotion accepted from randomized attestation shape
audit described as deployed attestation, external review, or open-world receipt faithfulness
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed artifact-attestation claim after deployed or externally reviewable attestation traces, independent observer routes, trap/challenge execution outside the producing component, verifier-quality evidence, provenance completeness review, residuals, non-claims, and external or independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: bounded deterministic local repository attestation only
fixed candidate pool and fixed seed, not an external random beacon
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent attestation, audit, verifier-quality, source-interpretation, external-project, or external human review
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable attestation service trace
no independent attestation route outside the producing component
non_claims: does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim

does not prove open-world receipt faithfulness, deployed attestation behavior, deployed audit behavior, verifier correctness, external project truth, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, deployment safety, or ASI
